section_name: Additional Lighting Equipment Authorized for Vehicles Operated by Justices of the Peace in Certain Circumstances
text:
(a) A vehicle operated by a justice of the peace in the course and scope of the justice’s duties as a coroner under Subchapter A , Chapter 49 (Inquests Upon Dead Bodies) , Code of Criminal Procedure, may be equipped with either:

(1) mounted signal lamps that comply with the requirements of Section 547.702 (Additional Equipment Requirements for Authorized Emergency Vehicles) (c); or

(2) a signal lamp that is temporarily attached to the vehicle roof and flashes red and blue lights visible at a distance of at least 500 feet in normal sunlight.

(b) A justice of the peace may use the lighting equipment described by Subsection (a) only when:

(1) necessary to warn other vehicle operators or pedestrians of the approach of a vehicle operated by a justice of the peace; and

(2) operating the vehicle in the course and scope of the justice’s duties relating to an inquest conducted under Subchapter A , Chapter 49 (Inquests Upon Dead Bodies) , Code of Criminal Procedure.
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 7
### Chapter 547. Vehicle Equip.
##### § 547.752 — Additional Lighting Equipment Authorized for Vehicles Operated by Justices of the Peace in Certain Circumstances

(a) A vehicle operated by a justice of the peace in the course and scope of the justice’s duties as a coroner under Subchapter A , Chapter 49 (Inquests Upon Dead Bodies) , Code of Criminal Procedure, may be equipped with either:

    (1) mounted signal lamps that comply with the requirements of Section 547.702 (Additional Equipment Requirements for Authorized Emergency Vehicles) (c); or

    (2) a signal lamp that is temporarily attached to the vehicle roof and flashes red and blue lights visible at a distance of at least 500 feet in normal sunlight.

(b) A justice of the peace may use the lighting equipment described by Subsection (a) only when:

    (1) necessary to warn other vehicle operators or pedestrians of the approach of a vehicle operated by a justice of the peace; and

    (2) operating the vehicle in the course and scope of the justice’s duties relating to an inquest conducted under Subchapter A , Chapter 49 (Inquests Upon Dead Bodies) , Code of Criminal Procedure.

*Added by Acts 2023, 88th Leg., R.S., Ch. 727 (H.B.2616), Sec. 2, eff. September 1, 2023.*

subsections.label: a
subsections.text: A vehicle operated by a justice of the peace in the course and scope of the justice’s duties as a coroner under Subchapter A , Chapter 49 (Inquests Upon Dead Bodies) , Code of Criminal Procedure, may be equipped with either:
mounted signal lamps that comply with the requirements of Section 547.702 (Additional Equipment Requirements for Authorized Emergency Vehicles) (c); or
a signal lamp that is temporarily attached to the vehicle roof and flashes red and blue lights visible at a distance of at least 500 feet in normal sunlight.

subsections.label: b
subsections.text: A justice of the peace may use the lighting equipment described by Subsection (a) only when:
necessary to warn other vehicle operators or pedestrians of the approach of a vehicle operated by a justice of the peace; and
operating the vehicle in the course and scope of the justice’s duties relating to an inquest conducted under Subchapter A , Chapter 49 (Inquests Upon Dead Bodies) , Code of Criminal Procedure.
